Title: OsFT coordinates photosynthetic stability and carotenoid metabolism to regulate tillering in rice

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Maintains PSII integrity and photosynthetic stability through interaction with OsFT; genetic disruption aggravates seedling chlorosis and growth defects in the ft background
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsFtsh2 was identified as an OsFT-interacting protein involved in PSII repair. 2. OsFT and OsFtsh2 co-localize in chloroplasts and physically interact in Y2H, BiFC, LCI, and Co-IP assays. 3. ft/ftsh2 double mutants showed enhanced defects versus single mutants, supporting additive roles in maintaining the photosynthetic system.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; subcellular localization; BiFC; LCI; Co-IP; CRISPR-Cas9 mutagenesis; double-mutant phenotyping
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubi promoter (fusion constructs); double CaMV35S promoter for localization/BiFC vectors
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High light context discussed for PSII repair; seedling stage phenotyping
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes albino lethality in rice seedlings per prior studies; double mutant shows more severe seedling defects
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although not a carotenoid enzyme or regulator, OsFtsh2 is a core experimentally tested interactor linking OsFT-dependent carotenoid/photosynthesis phenotypes to PSII protein renewal
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential complementary target to stabilize photosynthesis, but severe loss-of-function phenotypes caution against disruptive editing

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core carotenoid pathway upstream of all-trans-β-carotene and xanthophyll/ABA/SL precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes a carotenoid desaturation step at the ζ-carotene stage in the carotenoid biosynthetic pathway, supporting production of downstream carotenoids including all-trans-β-carotene and xanthophyll-cycle pigments
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: more desaturated carotenoid intermediates downstream of ζ-carotene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic light harvesting/photoprotection and hormone precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in stem, leaf and sheath; lower in panicle and root
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in stem, leaf and sheath; lower in panicle and root
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes carotenoid accumulation, maintains photosynthetic stability, increases ABA, modulates SL, and enhances tillering, drought tolerance, and yield when overexpressed
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Map-based cloning identified OsFT/OsZDS as LOC_Os07g10490 underlying the zebra-leaf few-tiller mutant. 2. Loss of OsFT reduced pigment content, lutein-cycle metabolites, all-trans-β-carotene, photosystem proteins, and tiller number. 3. Overexpression improved photosynthetic parameters, high-light energy utilization, drought tolerance, and yield, while altering ABA/SL balance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; complementation; overexpression; qRT-PCR; GUS assay; TEM; western blot of photosystem proteins; metabolite profiling (ABA, SL, all-trans-β-carotene, xanthophyll-cycle compounds); RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (12 Illumina HiSeq libraries); accession number NA
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Ubi promoter (constitutive) for overexpression; native OsFT promoter for GUS reporter
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High light treatment; drought field treatment
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression increased tiller number and yield per plant but decreased thousand-grain weight, grain length, and grain width
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper states OsFT is a new allele of OsZDS and links carotenoid metabolism with photosystem stability and hormone crosstalk; article text contains some inconsistent wording about intermediate carotenoid identities, but OsFT is clearly assigned as ZDS
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for breeding/engineering drought-resistant, high-yield rice with improved photosynthetic efficiency; should balance yield gain against reduced grain size traits

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Core carotenoid pathway downstream of OsFT/ZDS
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes carotenoid isomerization in the carotenoid biosynthesis pathway, supporting formation of all-trans carotenoid intermediates and downstream β-carotene-derived products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Photosynthetic light harvesting/photoprotection and hormone precursor supply
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation reduces pigment and all-trans-β-carotene accumulation and alters ABA/SL balance, increasing tiller number; its genetic interaction with OsFT partially restores ft tillering phenotype in the double mutant
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PGL13, a CRTISO enzyme in the carotenoid pathway, was analyzed genetically together with OsFT. 2. pgl13 mutants showed increased tiller number but reduced pigments, all-trans-β-carotene, ABA, and SL. 3. Double mutant ft/pgl13 showed partial phenotypic rescue, indicating functional synergy between consecutive carotenoid pathway steps.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant phenotyping; double-mutant analysis with ft; pigment and metabolite profiling; qRT-PCR of ABA/SL pathway genes; overexpression comparison (PGL13-OE1 mentioned)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core pathway gene because dedicated mutant and double-mutant experiments in Results directly test carotenoid-pathway effects on tillering
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be useful for manipulating tiller architecture through carotenoid-hormone crosstalk, but effects on pigment accumulation should be considered
